Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status

R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Team Roster

Name: Olivia Brooks     | Title: Solutions Architect    | Email: o.brooks@novabridge.com

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Projected additional Q3 overruns:

Note: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
CFO review scheduled: 2026-07-18
Action required: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT
NovaBridge Solutions Inc. ("NovaBridge") and Meridian Group LLC ("Meridian")
Executed: 2026-03-01

[EXCERPT — Selected Sections]

Section 4.2 — Payment Schedule and Milestone Definitions
  Milestone 1: Requirements sign-off and architecture approval — $85,000 (PAID 2026-04-15)
  Milestone 2: API Gateway delivery and client acceptance — $95,000 (PAID 2026-06-30)
  Milestone 3: Full system delivery and UAT sign-off — $120,000 (due upon UAT completion)
  Milestone 4: 3-month post-launch hypercare support — $40,000 (due 90 days post-launch)
  Total contract value: $340,000

Section 7.1 — Delivery Penalties
  Delays exceeding 30 calendar days beyond agreed milestone dates incur a penalty of
  1.5% of the outstanding milestone value per week, up to a maximum of 15%.
  Milestone 3 agreed delivery date: 2026-09-15.

Section 9.3 — Demo Requirements
  NovaBridge shall provide a functional product demonstration no later than 30 days prior
  to UAT commencement. The demo must include: (a) live data ingestion using
  client-provided sample data, (b) end-to-end pipeline execution, and
  (c) dashboard visualization with real data. Use of mock or synthetic data
  does not satisfy this requirement.

Section 11 — Intellectual Property
  All custom code, configurations, and documentation developed under this agreement
  become the exclusive property of Meridian Group upon receipt of final payment.

Section 14 — Confidentiality
  Both parties agree to keep the terms of this agreement strictly confidential.
  Disclosure to any third party requires written consent from both parties.

Pending items:

- CEO sign-off required before counter is submitted
- HR to confirm headcount forecast for space planning (requested 2026-07-08)

Decision deadline: 2026-08-01 (landlord requires 5-month notice for non-renewal)

Project Helios Team — Mid-Year Performance Reviews 2026
Reviewing Manager: Derek Okafor (Technical Lead)
Review period: January–June 2026

---
Amir Hassan — Senior Backend Engineer
Rating: Exceeds Expectations
Comments: Delivered data ingestion architecture ahead of schedule. Strong systems thinker
with excellent cross-team communication. Recommend for Senior II promotion consideration in H2.

---
Liu Wei — Backend Engineer
Rating: Meets Expectations

---

---

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between
NovaBridge Solutions Inc. ("Disclosing Party") and [VENDOR NAME] ("Receiving Party").

1. Confidential Information
The Receiving Party agrees to hold in strict confidence all technical, business,
and operational information disclosed by the Disclosing Party, including but not
limited to source code, system architecture, client data, and business processes.

2. Term
This Agreement shall remain in effect for three (3) years from the date of signature,
unless terminated earlier by written agreement of both parties.

3. Permitted Disclosures
The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information only to employees with a
demonstrated need to know, provided such employees are bound by equivalent obligations.

4. Return of Materials
Upon request or termination, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or certify
destruction of all Confidential Information and copies thereof.

5. Governing Law
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties execute this Agreement as of the date below.

NovaBridge Solutions Inc.

input.content:
================================================================================
  PROJECT HELIOS — COMPREHENSIVE PROJECT HEALTH BRIEFING
  Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
  Date: 2026-07-08
  Classification: INTERNAL — Leadership Eyes Only
================================================================================

1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Project Helios is currently ON TRACK with minor-to-moderate risks. The API
gateway has been delivered and accepted (Milestone 2 paid). Core modules are
advancing through Sprint 14, with a critical Meridian product demo scheduled
for 2026-07-25. Two high-severity open defects and three overdue Q2
deliverables require active management. Budget is $20K over Q2 plan, with
projected H2 overruns of ~$47K that cannot be passed to the client per MSA
Section 4.2. UAT has already consumed a 1-week extension; further slippage
beyond 2 weeks risks pushing launch into Q4 and triggering Section 7.1
delivery penalties.


2. CURRENT PROGRESS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Overall Status: ON TRACK (minor risks) — Week of 2026-07-06

2.1 Milestone Progress
  - Data ingestion module ............... 85% complete  (target: 2026-07-18)
  - API gateway ......................... 100% complete  (DELIVERED / Milestone 2 PAID)
  - Client dashboard .................... 60% complete   (target: 2026-07-22)
  - Integration testing ................. 40% complete

2.2 Q2 Deliverables Status
  DONE:
    - Requirements documentation v1.0
    - System architecture design
    - API gateway implementation
    - Development environment setup
    - Data schema finalization

  IN PROGRESS:
    - Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
    - Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)

  OVERDUE:
    - Integration test suite setup
        Was due: 2026-06-30 | Rescheduled: 2026-07-20
    - UAT environment provisioning
        Was due: 2026-07-01 | Blocked by Meridian IT access
    - Performance benchmarking report
        Was due: 2026-07-05 | Owner: Derek Okafor (not yet started)

2.3 Sprint 14 Snapshot (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18)
  Goals: Complete dashboard module v1; resolve data format inconsistencies;
         begin UAT preparation.

  Task Status:
    HEL-241  Dashboard data table component       Fatima Al-Rashid   In Progress
    HEL-242  Dashboard chart rendering module     Tomas Rivera       In Progress
    HEL-243  Date format normalization            Amir Hassan        BLOCKED
    HEL-244  Integration tests: auth flows        Kenji Watanabe     Done
    HEL-245  Integration tests: data pipeline     Kenji Watanabe     In Progress
    HEL-246  UAT environment server provisioning  Nadia Petrov       At Risk
    HEL-247  Performance benchmark: dashboard     Derek Okafor       Not Started
    HEL-248  GeoSync license renewal              Raj Patel          In Progress

2.4 QA / Testing Status (Sprint 14)
  Executed: 10 of 15 test cases
  Results:  8 PASS, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run / in progress

  Open High-Severity Defects:
    HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input.
               Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
               Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian clean sample
               data (due 2026-07-15).

    HEL-D-015  Large-file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
               Pipeline crashes at ~210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
               Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

  Auth flows suite: 4/4 PASS. Dashboard suite: largely incomplete (1/4 done).


3. KEY RISKS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Source: Risk Register (last reviewed 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen, PM)

  ID     Description                              L        I       Score  Status
  -----  ---------------------------------------  -------  ------  -----  ------
  R-01   Meridian source data quality issues      High     High    9      ACTIVE
         Confirmed date-format blocker in Sprint 14. Mitigation owner:
         Amir Hassan. Meridian committed clean sample data by 2026-07-15.

  R-02   GeoSync license not renewed before demo  Medium   High    6      ACTIVE
         Legal review in progress. Target resolution: 2026-07-14.
         Mitigation owner: Raj Patel. Unresolved license blocks mapping
         features in the 07-25 demo.

  R-03   Dashboard performance on large datasets  Medium   High    6      ACTIVE
         Benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started. Derek Okafor owns.
         Meridian sponsor raised explicit concern at 07-08 sync meeting.

  R-04   UAT timeline slippage                    High     Medium  6      ACTIVE
         1-week extension already consumed (now Aug 17–28). Further
         delay of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4 and triggers
         Section 7.1 penalty review. Owner: Sarah Chen.

  R-05   Key person dependency: Derek Okafor      Low      High    3      MONITORED
         No backup tech lead identified. Single-point-of-failure risk
         for architecture decisions and performance work.

  R-06   Meridian IT environment access delays    Medium   Medium  4      ACTIVE
         Awaiting IT infrastructure diagram (Priya Kapoor, due 07-12).
         UAT access committed by Meridian for 2026-07-19. Owner: Nadia Petrov.

  R-07   Scope creep from Meridian feature reqs   Low      Medium  2      MONITORED
         3 informal requests logged; not yet escalated. Owner: Raj Patel.

  R-08   Cloud cost overrun                       Low      Low     1      MONITORED
  R-09   Third-party API deprecation              Very Low Medium  1      MONITORED

Highest-priority risks for leadership focus: R-01, R-02, R-03, R-04.


4. UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Source: Q3 Roadmap Draft (updated 2026-07-06) | Target launch: 2026-10-01

4.1 July — Complete System Integration
  - Finalize data ingestion module ............... target 2026-07-18
  - Complete client dashboard v1 ................. target 2026-07-22
  - Integration test sign-off .................... target 2026-07-25

4.2 July 25 — Product Demo for Meridian (CONTRACTUAL — MSA Section 9.3)
  Requirements (non-negotiable):
    (a) Live data ingestion using Meridian-provided sample data
    (b) End-to-end pipeline execution
    (c) Dashboard visualization with real data
  Mock/synthetic data does NOT satisfy the contractual requirement.
  James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor) reconfirmed this at the
  2026-07-08 sync. Demo agenda due to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (Sarah Chen).

4.3 August 17–28 — User Acceptance Testing (Meridian Group)
  - Deploy to UAT environment
  - Support Meridian user testing sessions
  - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)
  Note: Already extended by 1 week from original plan.

4.4 August–September — Performance Hardening
  - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
  - Ingestion throughput: 500k records/min sustained
  - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

4.5 September — Launch Preparation
  - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
  - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
  - Final client training sessions

4.6 Proposed Q3 Feature Additions (subject to change-control approval)
  - Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
  - Email alerting for pipeline failures
  - Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

4.7 Contract Milestone Timeline (MSA Section 4.2)
  Milestone 1: Requirements + architecture ........ $85,000   PAID (2026-04-15)
  Milestone 2: API Gateway delivery ............... $95,000   PAID (2026-06-30)
  Milestone 3: Full system delivery + UAT ......... $120,000  Due upon UAT completion
               Agreed delivery date: 2026-09-15
  Milestone 4: 3-month post-launch hypercare ...... $40,000   Due 90 days post-launch
  Total contract value: $340,000


5. ISSUES REQUIRING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

5.1 BUDGET OVERRUN — ACTION REQUIRED
  Q2 actuals: $322,000 vs. budget $302,000 (+$20,000 / +6.6%).
  Drivers: unplanned May sprint (+$14.2K engineering), GeoSync scoping
  subcontractors (+$6.8K), early load-test cloud environment (+$3.5K).

  Projected additional Q3 overruns:
    - GeoSync license (if not renewed before expiry) .... ~$12,000
    - UAT extension (1 additional week already taken) ... ~$15,000
    - Estimated total H2 projected overrun .............. ~$47,000

  CRITICAL: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns CANNOT be passed
  to Meridian Group. CFO review is scheduled for 2026-07-18. Budget
  reconciliation plan is due to the CFO by 2026-07-20.
  → Leadership decision needed on absorption strategy and any scope
    trade-offs to contain H2 spend.

5.2 CONTRACTUAL DEMO RISK (2026-07-25)
  The July 25 demo is a contractual obligation under MSA Section 9.3 and
  must use live Meridian data. Three blockers threaten readiness:
    1. Date-format defect (HEL-D-014) — blocked on Meridian sample data
       due 2026-07-15. Only 10 days of buffer if data arrives on time.
    2. GeoSync license renewal — must close by 2026-07-14 or mapping
       features are unavailable for the demo.
    3. Dashboard performance benchmark (HEL-247) — not started; Meridian
       sponsor has already flagged load-time concerns.
  → Leadership should ensure Legal prioritizes GeoSync review and that
    Derek Okafor has capacity to complete benchmarking before the demo.

5.3 DELIVERY PENALTY EXPOSURE (MSA Section 7.1)
  Milestone 3 agreed delivery date: 2026-09-15.
  Delays exceeding 30 calendar days incur 1.5% of outstanding milestone
  value per week (max 15%). Outstanding Milestone 3 value: $120,000.
  Weekly penalty if triggered: $1,800. UAT has already used its 1-week
  buffer. Any further UAT or integration slippage of >2 weeks pushes
  launch into Q4 and activates penalty review.
  → Leadership should treat the UAT window (Aug 17–28) as immovable and
    escalate any emerging blockers immediately.

5.4 KEY-PERSON / CAPACITY RISK
  Derek Okafor (Tech Lead) owns architecture, performance benchmarking,
  GeoSync coordination support, and is the sole technical escalation
  path. No backup tech lead is identified (R-05). Additionally, Tomas
  Rivera (Frontend) is on a performance improvement plan (initiated
  2026-06-15) with two missed Q2 sprint commitments — relevant because
  he owns the chart rendering module (HEL-242) needed for the demo.
  → Leadership should consider identifying a deputy tech lead and
    confirming frontend coverage contingency for the demo.

5.5 MERIDIAN DEPENDENCY CHAIN
  Several critical path items depend on Meridian deliverables:
    - Clean sample dataset .............. due 2026-07-15 (Priya Kapoor / Meridian IT)
    - IT infrastructure diagram ......... due 2026-07-12
    - UAT environment access ............ committed for 2026-07-19
  Missed Meridian dates cascade into our blockers (R-01, R-06) and
  ultimately into demo and UAT readiness.
  → Recommend leadership-level outreach to James Whitfield (Meridian
    Project Sponsor) if Meridian dates slip, to keep escalation paths
    open and documented.


6. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS & DECISION LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Action                                          Owner              Due
  ----------------------------------------------  -----------------  ----------
  Resolve GeoSync license renewal                 Raj Patel / Legal  2026-07-14
  Receive Meridian clean sample data              Meridian IT        2026-07-15
  Fix date normalization defect (HEL-D-014)       Amir Hassan        After data
  Investigate memory overflow (HEL-D-015)         Amir Hassan        ASAP
  Complete dashboard performance benchmark        Derek Okafor       2026-07-18
  Send draft demo agenda to Meridian              Sarah Chen         2026-07-18
  CFO review of Q2 overrun                        Finance / CFO      2026-07-18
  Budget reconciliation plan to CFO               PM / Finance       2026-07-20
  Integration test sign-off                       Team               2026-07-25
  Product demo for Meridian                       Full team          2026-07-25

  Decisions Needed from Leadership:
  1. Approve budget absorption plan for ~$47K H2 projected overrun
     (or authorize scope reductions to offset).
  2. Confirm Legal prioritization of GeoSync license by 2026-07-14.
  3. Endorse identification of backup tech lead to mitigate R-05.
  4. Authorize escalation protocol with Meridian sponsor if sample data
     or IT access dates are missed.


7. BOTTOM LINE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Helios remains deliverable on the 2026-10-01 launch target, but the path
is narrowing. The next 17 days (through the 07-25 demo) are critical:
data-quality blockers, license renewal, and performance benchmarking must
all clear. Budget pressure is real and non-billable. Leadership engagement
on the four decision items above will materially improve the probability
of on-time, on-contract delivery.

Prepared from: project_helios_status.txt, risk_register.txt, q3_roadmap_draft.txt,
q2_deliverables_checklist.txt, meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt, helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt,
sprint_14_task_assignments.txt, budget_vs_actuals_q2.xlsx, meridian_contract_signed.pdf,
team_roster.txt, helios_architecture_overview.txt

================================================================================
  End of Briefing
================================================================================

content.text:
The comprehensive project health briefing has been saved as `project_health_briefing.txt`. Here's what it covers:

**1. Current Progress**
- Overall ON TRACK with minor risks
- API gateway delivered (Milestone 2 paid); ingestion at 85%, dashboard at 60%, integration testing at 40%
- Sprint 14 task status, including blocked date-normalization work and unstarted performance benchmarks
- QA: 2 high-severity open defects (date formats affecting ~40% of Meridian data; memory overflow on large files)

**2. Key Risks**
- Top risks: Meridian data quality (score 9), GeoSync license before demo (6), dashboard performance (6), UAT slippage (6)
- Key-person dependency on Derek Okafor with no backup tech lead

**3. Upcoming Q3 Commitments**
- July 25 Meridian product demo (contractual — live data required)
- UAT Aug 17–28 (already extended 1 week)
- Performance hardening and launch prep targeting 2026-10-01 go-live
- Milestone 3 ($120K) due upon UAT completion; agreed date 2026-09-15

**4. Issues for Leadership Attention**
- **Budget**: Q2 +$20K overrun; ~$47K projected H2 overruns that cannot be billed to Meridian; reconciliation plan due to CFO by 07-20
- **Demo risk**: Three blockers (data formats, GeoSync license, performance) threaten the contractual 07-25 demo
- **Penalty exposure**: Further UAT/integration slippage of >2 weeks activates MSA Section 7.1 penalties
- **Capacity**: Tech lead single-point-of-failure; one frontend engineer on a PIP who owns demo-critical chart work
- **Meridian dependencies**: Sample data (07-15), IT diagram (07-12), UAT access (07-19)

Four specific leadership decisions are flagged at the end of the briefing.
